conflicts.description: The prior sheet placed Meridian's contract meeting about one week after Kai's crash / roughly three weeks after arrival, but the manuscript dates it to a Tuesday in late February after a late-January start.
conflicts.canonical_value: Meridian's delegation and contract meeting occur in late February 2038, several weeks after arrival and after Kai's February crash.
conflicts.resolution_note: Absolute month language in chapter_08 and the two-week marker at Kai's crash are stronger than the old approximate sheet timing, so the event timing was updated.

conflicts.description: The prior sheet placed Lena's patch installation about five to six weeks after arrival, but chapter sequencing and dated markers place it in the later spring-thaw window.
conflicts.canonical_value: Lena's patch belongs in late March 2038, roughly eight weeks after arrival.
conflicts.resolution_note: This timing is an inference from the late-January start, the two-week crash marker, chapter_14's 'six weeks ago' reference to Kai's crash, and chapter_15's 'four months to the opening ceremony.' That support is better than the old estimate, so the sheet was updated.

conflicts.description: The prior sheet treated Kai and Lena as already-established romantic partners, but the manuscript makes the romance a mid-book development that becomes explicit in chapter_11.
conflicts.canonical_value: Kai and Lena become romantic partners from chapter_11 onward; the sheet now records that as a state transition instead of implying the relationship existed at story start.
conflicts.resolution_note: The relationship change drives later behavior and should not be back-projected into earlier chapters, so the sheet was corrected for timing.

conflicts.description: Chapter 8 says Jin-seo's brother Jun-ho was a triathlete killed during the cycling stage of a cascade failure, while chapter 12 says he was a 1500 runner whose legs stopped at 800m.
conflicts.canonical_value: Keep only the stable canon that Jin-seo's brother died in a 2034 enhancement-cascade failure.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter 8 is more scene-embedded, but the manuscript never reconciles the contradiction. The context pack also only supports the general 2034 death, so event-specific details stay non-canonical and the sheet keeps the broader fact.

conflicts.description: Chapter 1 quantifies Kai's proprioception gap at roughly 40-50 milliseconds, while chapter 3 charts the same drift across three sessions as 14/22/31 milliseconds.
conflicts.canonical_value: Kai's proprioception drift is widening and noticeable; no numeric value is treated as canonical in the sheet.
conflicts.resolution_note: The manuscript does not reconcile whether these are the same metric or two logging views. Canonical stability is better served by keeping the qualitative fact and dropping the conflicting numbers.
